heading: Bonnie Doon vs Bonshaw — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Bonnie Doon, Victoria and Bonshaw, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Bonnie Doon has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Bonshaw (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Bonnie Doon or Bonshaw safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Bonnie Doon has a flood risk score of 31/100, while Bonshaw scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Bonnie Doon scores 29/100 for bushfire risk. Bonshaw scores 46/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Bonnie Doon or Bonshaw?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Bonnie Doon has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
